query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "code as agent harness" linguistics
rationale: The paper focuses on a specific system implementation (AutoMegaKernel) for static checking and performance optimization of LLM inference, which does not address the user's question regarding the "linguistics" of the "Code as Agent Harness" framework or the theoretical/linguistic extensions of that specific prior work. While it mentions an "agent harness," it uses the term in a distinct, low-level systems context (CUDA kernel scheduling) rather than the linguistic or high-level agent-architecture
